body.u: When I was fifteen I dreamed about being a good hairdresser so that it could lead on to better things. I hated school but I didn’t really want to leave. Like a lot of us I was scared but I knew I had to leave at some time so I left at the end of the 5th year. I started work in a small hairdressing salon with a very low wage and doing all the jobs no-one else wanted to do. I often thought of giving it up and working in a factory but I know? Couldn’t do that. The thing I hated most was going to college one every week. While I was at school I really dreaded doing the apprentashipapprenticeship and thought the time would drag by but it didn’t and soon I was a qualified hairdresser. I was offered a job in a large salon and the pay was good.
I got married when I was 23 to someone with a fair bit of money.
We never had any children and I continued working until I had enough money to open a salon of my own.

My aim was to take as many juniors as I could because through my own experience I found it difficult to get a job with a manager willing to train me. After we trained a lot of apprentices I started taking on regular staff. I had a fantastic house with every thingeverything I’d ever wanted in it. This was partly the reason I never had any children, I didn’t want everything messed up. Another was that I didn’t like them. My husband and I lead our own lives and only occasionally went out together. We didn’t have rows because we didn’t see that much of each other. He knew I only married him for his money and accepted it. We mostly went around with our own groups of friends meeting different people. We might as well not have got married really but we never thought about it at the time.

He died when he was 45 and though we never behaved like we were married I missed him more than I ever thought I would, but then I was always like that I never really wanted anything until it was gone. Just like the salon after I retired and sold it I missed it but still have the house which is my most treasured possession. I’m not affraidafraid of dying in fact a I’ve always looked forward to it and I do believe there’s something after death otherwise living’s not worth it.
